svg.text: The science. The strategy. The execution.

text.tspan: Everything you need to know about sustainable fat loss —
without the bro-science. Calorie targets, deficit mechanics,
protein strategy, body recomposition, and the exact framework
HOW TO CALCULATE YOUR CALORIE TARGET
DEFICIT SIZING: HOW AGGRESSIVE IS TOO AGGRESSIVE
Every fat loss plan starts here. Not with a meal plan. Not with a food list.
With a number — your Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE).

svg.text: This gives you your Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR). Multiply by activity factor for TDEE.

text.tspan: Male, 85kg, 180cm, 32 years old, trains 4× per week
This is maintenance. To lose fat, we subtract from here.

svg.text: We don't trust calculators alone. Start with TDEE estimate, track intake for 2 weeks, then adjust based on real data.

text.tspan: Most people overestimate activity level and undercount calories by 20-40%.
Start conservative. Adjust based on results — not feelings.
In a deficit, protein is the difference between losing fat and losing muscle.
Get this wrong and you'll shrink instead of sharpen.

svg.text: The research-backed target range for a deficit

text.tspan: Distribute protein across 3-5 meals. Aim for 25-50g per meal to maximize
muscle protein synthesis (MPS). The anabolic window is real — it's just
wider than bros think. Hit your daily total, and hit it consistently.

svg.text: TOP PROTEIN SOURCES FOR BUSY PROFESSIONALS

text.tspan: Chicken breast — 31g per 100g | Greek yogurt — 10g per 100g | Eggs — 6g each
Whey protein — 25g per scoop | Lean beef — 26g per 100g | Cottage cheese — 11g per 100g
Tuna — 30g per can | Turkey — 29g per 100g | Tofu — 8g per 100g

svg.text: Protein is the one macro we never cut. Fats and carbs flex around it. Protein stays fixed — always.

text.tspan: The size of your deficit determines rate of loss, muscle risk, and sustainability.
Go too hard, and your body fights back. Go too light, and nothing happens.
Metabolic adaptation. Muscle loss. Hormonal disruption. Deficits over 40% trigger your body's survival response —
cortisol spikes, thyroid downregulation, and increased hunger hormones. You lose weight, but you lose the wrong kind.
TDEE of 2,800 cal/day × 25% deficit = 700 calorie deficit
Target intake: 2,100 calories/day → Expected loss: ~1.4 lbs/week

svg.text: Start at 20%. Measure for 2-3 weeks. If progress is steady and energy is stable, stay. If not, adjust by 5% in either direction.

text.tspan: Lose fat and build muscle at the same time. It's not a myth — but it requires
Recomp is slow. The scale may not change for weeks, even as your body transforms. Trust measurements,
progress photos, and strength numbers — not the scale.
Plateaus are normal. They are not permission to panic-cut calories.
Here's the diagnostic framework we use at Flint.
02 Has it been less than 2 weeks?
Water fluctuations mask real progress. Wait.
Poor sleep = elevated cortisol = water retention.
Cortisol drives water retention and stalls.
Training drives the partition of nutrients to muscle.
Only cut from fats or carbs — never protein.
Minimum viable change. Reassess in 2 weeks.
Increase expenditure instead of decreasing intake.
Resets hormones, reduces adaptation. Then resume.
1-2 higher-carb days per week at maintenance.
Leptin boost. Glycogen refill. Mental break.
You don't need to weigh every gram forever. You need a system that fits your life.

svg.text: Full track for the first phase. Transition to hybrid. The goal is building intuition — not creating a lifelong dependency on an app.

text.tspan: This guide gives you the framework. Flint gives you the execution.
Custom programming. Precision nutrition. Accountability that works.
